title: Paranoia on buying Eva DVDs online
display_title: Paranoia on buying Eva DVDs online
wikitext:
[]: Paranoia on buying Eva DVD's online             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Thu Jul 19, 2012 3:04 pm I haven't had much experience with buying Used copies of things online really and i'm not much of a fan of buying Used things in general. I've always had this notion that buying used things online aswell was always very risky so i'm quite paranoid on what DVD's I should buy for End of Evangelion and Death and Rebirth online and sent to the UK. I've only really been looking at Ebay, Amazon and Play.com but I was hoping that perhaps people here with experience of buying merchandise and used Eva items online can give me advice on what copies to buy of End of Eva and Death and Rebirth? Thanks! http://www.amazon.co.uk/gp/product/B000083EEH/ref=s9_simh_gw_p74_d1_g74_i2?pf_rd_m=A3P5ROKL5A1OLE&pf_rd_s=center-2&pf_rd_r=0WZ42CY990RN4XKNPEDW&pf_rd_t=101&pf_rd_p=467128533&pf_rd_i=468294 http://www.amazon.co.uk/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Death-Rebirth/dp/B00006IIZ4/ref=pd_sim_d_h__3

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Jul 19, 2012 4:10 pm Yeah, those are the right two items for the UK. BTW, this auction looks good for The End of Evangelion, as it has a picture included.

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Thu Jul 19, 2012 4:12 pm svenge wrote: Yeah, those are the right two items for the UK.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Jul 19, 2012 4:18 pm Eliaskar wrote: That isn't the only thing though, I find it difficult to trust all the different people selling Used copies. Especially the one selling Death and Rebirth for 1p.

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Thu Jul 19, 2012 4:38 pm svenge wrote: Death and Rebirth really doesn't have nearly as much value on the used market as The End of Evangelion for a few reasons (namely that half of it is a recap of the TV series and the other half is duplicated and extended upon in The End of Evangelion). The 1p thing is a bit odd though. In the US, the lowest copy on Amazon is $6.50 (or about £4). Maybe there's a relative glut of copies in the UK. If you want to see the physical item you're purchasing, eBay's your best bet. I found this auction of Death and Rebirth with a photograph of the actual item. There's also this auction of the Manga Force Magazine variant, which should also be suitable.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Jul 19, 2012 5:07 pm Eliaskar wrote: Hm, I'm not sure if i'd consider buying those though because of the quality of the items and I don't really like the look of the Manga Force variant. I'm kinda picky unfortunately with these sort of things...

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Thu Jul 19, 2012 5:21 pm svenge wrote: That's OK. It's not like I would get a commission out of it anyhow. :) Just trying to help out...

[]:             Quote Post by transcendent12 » Mon Jul 23, 2012 8:35 pm Dealing with the quality of End of Eva english releases is probably the hardest part of making sure everything you have is of decent quality. To keep things simple the normal Manga entertainment EoE release (without the manga force writing at the bottom) is the definitive english release, has the commentary and english subs. However the video quality is somewhat poor. Japanese feature films collectors DTS edition is the best official video version but only comes with Japanese subs. Ignore everything here on unless you are techy... SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Wed Aug 22, 2012 5:09 pm I'm fine with buying used if necessary, but I need a little help. I'm pretty confused on all the different releases for the original series, and I'm interested in finally buying a set (and clearing my hard drive space). What set/release can match this criteria: -DVD -Has subtitles that are good -Japanese and English sound -Directors Cut episodes for any episode that has it A nice box and DVD inserts would be cool but aren't necessary. So which should I get? Also, any recommended release of EoE (based on the same criteria)?

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Wed Aug 22, 2012 5:29 pm Platinum Complete Collection is pretty good for all that, except it doesn't have the End of Evangelion movie. It does have both the original and director's cuts of the episodes that have them (21 - 24). The subtitles are a little frustrating though; they don't always match the order of what's being said on-screen and also the background conversations [TV/NERV announcements and such] are sometimes subtitled and sometimes not; and the colors of the subtitles sometimes change between yellow and white even when it's the same person speaking. Overall I found that release pretty good though; surround sound on the Japanese track is awesome! As for End of Eva I'm not aware of multiple releases that have English. You're just stuck with the one released by Manga Entertainment years ago.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Wed Aug 22, 2012 5:30 pm Chroma wrote: -DVD -Has subtitles that are good -Japanese and English sound -Directors Cut episodes for any episode that has it

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 22, 2012 5:38 pm Chroma: Bumping this particular thread (which I wouldn't have expected to answer your questions) isn't quite what I had in mind. Looking in more appropriate ones, THEN bumping if you don't find what you need, is what I intended. http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10740/Best-DVD-set/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3439/Which-DVD-set-should-I-buy/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/7703/Best-DVD-Set/ The EoE issue is probably addressed in greater detail in one or all of those, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Wed Aug 22, 2012 11:13 pm Reichu wrote: Chroma: Bumping this particular thread (which I wouldn't have expected to answer your questions) isn't quite what I had in mind. Looking in more appropriate ones, THEN bumping if you don't find what you need, is what I intended. http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10740/Best-DVD-set/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3439/Which-DVD-set-should-I-buy/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/7703/Best-DVD-Set/ The EoE issue is probably addressed in greater detail in one or all of those, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Aug 22, 2012 11:18 pm Chroma wrote: Ooohhh. I apologize. My question was pretty much answered anyway, but I'll look into those threads for a bit more info and for what I should do for EoE and Death and Rebirth. Sorry for all the trouble.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Thu Aug 23, 2012 7:22 am svenge wrote: I'm currently working on a comprehensive guide to the Evangelion movies on DVD (the 1997 ones, not the current "Rebuild" tetralogy), so if you can hold tight you'll get some quality answers in a concise format shortly.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Aug 23, 2012 8:49 am svenge wrote: I'm currently working on a comprehensive guide to the Evangelion movies on DVD (the 1997 ones, not the current "Rebuild" tetralogy)

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Aug 23, 2012 1:43 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Is there something else to know about them in addition to the infos that you've collected in your website?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Aug 23, 2012 2:40 pm svenge wrote: It's going to be more of a in-depth examination of the details for all the Japanese and US theatrical DVDs, as well as what can be done for those dissatisfied with the US release.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Aug 23, 2012 10:58 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Every time I stare at the track list of that DVD I remain astonished, I think that they take more than 2.5 mbps.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Aug 23, 2012 11:38 pm svenge wrote: If you asked me to provide a ranked list of the problems that the Manga EoE DVD had, insufficient video bitrate wouldn't be very high on it.
